name: Smoked Mackerel with Nuts, Grapes & Brown Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the brown rice in at least double its volume of fast boiling salted water.
2. Once cooked, refresh the rice and dress it with 3 tablespoons lemon juice, 1 tablespoon sunflower oil, plenty of salt, and pepper.
3. Stir in the chopped hazelnuts and 5-6 tablespoons of fresh mint or watercress (watercress is good but mint is even better).
4. Press the mixture firmly into a lightly oiled ring mold and cover; chill for several hours.
5. Just before serving, halve the grapes and toss them with the juice of half a lemon plus 1 tablespoon oil, along with some mint or watercress sprigs.
6. Skin, bone, and flake the smoked mackerel, then gently mix it into the rice mixture.
7. Unmold the ring of rice, placing some of the fish and grape mixture in the center. Garnish with sprigs of mint or watercress.
8. Serve the remaining fish mixture in a separate bowl, and offer the well-chilled yoghurt in another bowl.
